title: Add `uv check` to run `ty` from uv
overview: This PR adds `uv check`, a new subcommand that downloads and runs `ty` (Astral's Python type checker) against the current project. The implementation follows the same pattern as `uv format`/`uv audit`: discover the project, optionally sync the environment, fetch the binary from GitHub/Astral mirror, then exec it. Read outside-in: CLI contract → settings resolution → command dispatch → core implementation → binary download support → preview feature flag → tests.

chapters.title: uv check — CLI contract and user-facing args
chapters.summary: The new `ProjectCommand::Check(CheckArgs)` variant and its full `CheckArgs` struct define what users can pass to `uv check`.

chapters.title: uv check — settings resolution (CLI → typed struct)
chapters.summary: CheckSettings resolves the raw Clap args into a typed, normalized struct that the command handler consumes.

chapters.title: uv check — command dispatch in run_project
chapters.summary: The match arm in run_project wires CheckSettings into the check() command call.

chapters.title: uv check — core command implementation
chapters.summary: The async check() function: project discovery, optional env sync, ty download, and exec.

chapters.title: Binary download support for ty (uv-bin-install)
chapters.summary: Teaches the uv-bin-install crate about the ty binary: URL patterns, default version constraints, and mirror support.

chapters.title: PreviewFeature::Check — feature flag gate
chapters.summary: Registers 'check' as a named preview feature so the experimental warning can be suppressed with --preview-features check.

chapters.title: Test infrastructure and integration tests for uv check
chapters.summary: TestContext::check() helper and the full integration test suite covering the main scenarios.

units.layerReason: Outermost public contract — the Clap subcommand declaration
units.summary: Registers `check` as a project-level subcommand with the `uv help check` after-help link.

units.layerReason: CLI argument struct — the full user-visible API surface
units.summary: Full set of flags: extras/groups for env sync, --locked/--frozen/--no-sync, --python, --version (ty version), --extra-args (pass-through to ty), --no-project, --show-version. Mirrors the shape of `FormatArgs` but adds sync-related flags since check needs a live environment.

units.layerReason: Settings layer — translates CLI flags into resolved, conflict-checked values
units.summary: Introduces `CheckSettings` and its `resolve()` impl. Handles flag conflicts (locked vs frozen), dev/no-dev pair resolution, and propagates install mirrors and malware settings — the same resolution pattern used by sync/run.

units.layerReason: Entrypoint dispatch — bridges CLI to the async command function
units.summary: Resolves settings, checks refresh/offline conflicts, initialises the cache with combined refresh, then calls commands::check with all resolved arguments. Passes client_builder.subcommand(["check"]) so network attribution is correct.

units.layerReason: Module plumbing that makes the new check module visible
units.summary: Declares and re-exports the check module so the dispatch arm can call commands::check.

units.layerReason: Application logic — the actual implementation of the capability
units.summary: Implements the full check pipeline in one function: (1) warn if not in preview mode; (2) discover project or fall back to cwd; (3) warn when project-only flags are used without a project; (4) if a project was found, get-or-init the virtual env and run lock+sync (same path as `uv run`); (5) resolve ty version from BinVersion enum (default constraints >=0.0,<0.1, pinned, latest, or constraint); (6) download and cache ty via bin_install; (7) spawn `ty check` in the project root with VIRTUAL_ENV set, forwarding extra_args. The venv path is passed as VIRTUAL_ENV so ty can find installed stubs.

units.layerReason: Adapter/infrastructure — binary download plumbing
units.summary: Adds the `Ty` variant to the `Binary` enum, its name ("ty"), default constraints (>=0.0,<0.1), and the two URL constants TY_GITHUB_URL_PREFIX and TY_MIRROR_SUFFIX. The default constraint range mirrors how Ruff is pinned: a narrow range that allows patch updates without a uv release.

units.layerReason: Adapter — URL generation logic for ty artifacts
units.summary: Implements the same mirror-first, GitHub-fallback URL pattern that Ruff uses, but pointed at astral-sh/ty releases.

units.layerReason: Tests for the binary download layer
units.summary: Unit tests for ty download URL generation (custom mirror, default mirror + GitHub fallback) and for mirror URL rewriting, matching the existing test coverage for Ruff.

units.layerReason: Cross-cutting — preview gating infrastructure
units.summary: Adds Check = 1 << 33 to the PreviewFeature bitflag enum, maps it to the string "check" in as_str/FromStr, and tests the round-trip. Without this flag the command still works but prints an experimental warning.

units.layerReason: Test helper — shared test setup
units.summary: Adds context.check() mirroring context.format(): sets UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER to a future date so ty versions are resolvable in tests.

units.layerReason: Test registration
units.summary: Gates the check test module behind #[cfg(feature = "test-python")], matching the level of the audit tests.

units.layerReason: Integration test — happy path
units.summary: Basic success case: project with pyproject.toml, valid Python code, expects exit 0 and the experimental warning.

units.layerReason: Integration test — no-project fallback
units.summary: Verifies that check still runs (against cwd) when no pyproject.toml exists, and that project-only flags (--group, --frozen, --no-sync) are warned about and ignored.

units.layerReason: Integration test — --no-project flag
units.summary: Tests that --no-project skips project discovery even when a pyproject.toml is present with an unsatisfiable Python requirement, and that project-only flags generate warnings.

units.layerReason: Integration test — ty diagnostic output
units.summary: Verifies that ty's actual error output (invalid-assignment) is surfaced with exit code 1, confirming end-to-end integration with ty.

units.layerReason: Integration test — env sync + ty resolution
units.summary: Tests that uv syncs the declared dependency (iniconfig) into the venv and that ty resolves the import through VIRTUAL_ENV. Gated behind test-pypi.

units.layerReason: Integration test — ty unresolved-import diagnostic
units.summary: Verifies that an undeclared import produces a ty unresolved-import diagnostic, confirming the venv is correctly passed and ty inspects it.

units.layerReason: Snapshot test updates for CLI help text
units.summary: Snapshot updates adding 'check  Type check the project' to all help output variants.

units.layerReason: Snapshot test for preview feature enumeration
units.summary: Adds Check to the preview features list in the show_settings snapshot.
